relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The fifth trumpet opens abyssal imagery.
Smoke darkens perception.
Locust powers emerge from smoke.
The sealed are distinguished from the unsealed.
The harm is limited in time and scope.
Despair marks the depth of the sign.
The locusts appear like war-horses.
Their imagery is hybrid and unsettling.
Their sound is like many chariots.
Their stings carry limited authority.
Destruction is named Abaddon/Apollyon.
One woe passes; more remain.
The sixth trumpet sounds from the altar.
Bound powers at Euphrates are released.
Their timing is appointed.
An overwhelming cavalry number is heard.
Visionary horses carry fire, smoke, and sulfur imagery.
The signs expose destructive force.
Mouth and tail symbolize harmful power.
Yet the remaining humans do not turn from idols.
They do not turn from violent, occult, exploitative, or stealing patterns.

contemplative_note: Revelation 9 shows destruction unmasking itself, but the tragedy is refusal to turn from lifeless idols and harmful works.
